mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, tight labor market conditions signal peak cyclical demand, which historically precedes margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and a contraction in housing starts indicates a sectoral rotation from real estate to manufacturing, which historically drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as rate hike expectations accelerate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% MoM while HOUST falls > 3% MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict directionally correct SPX moves in more than 55% of historical instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the CPI index exceeds 260, aggressive monetary tightening suppresses corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL > 260 and FEDFUNDS rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate cash flows, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerates while SPX forward returns remain positive for three consecutive quarters, breaking the earnings-revision linkage.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand, which historically fuels consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative SPX returns in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds the threshold, indicating decoupling from equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises concurrently with rising federal funds rates, supply-side strength fails to offset higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 60% of historical episodes where both series move in the specified directions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market contraction where housing starts fall more than 10 percent in three months, consumer wealth effects reverse sharply, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as retail earnings disappoint.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral despite a >10% quarterly decline in housing starts, invalidating the wealth-effect transmission channel.
